faq.answer_1: If you are very eager to try out Raak while it is in alpha, <Link>let us know</Link>. We advice most of you to wait for the private beta. A more detailed roadmap will also be released by then.
faq.answer_2: Linear is amazing: their design and sync engine are aspirations for Raak. That said, Raak is focused on small companies with a single team, working on multiple projects. Our intuitive yet high density workspaces capture all tasks without the need to switch pages.
faq.answer_3: Though Pivotal Tracker (2006) has been <Link>shut down</Link>, it was a very effective way to communicate tasks. Raak is heavily inspired by Pivotal Tracker. Yet, Raak also does many things different: no comments nor notifications (inline mentioning is being considered though). A modern UI, more granular editing, a table view and dark mode.
faq.answer_4: At its core, Raak is just an issue tracking board. There will be no chat or comments in Raak itself. Rather, we will research how to best embed Raak into your existing project management workflow.
pricing.text_1: Ideal if you are just getting started.
pricing.text_2: For those that have multiple projects in production.
text_1: Raak is a fast, high-density issue tracker designed for small teams that manage multiple projects simultaneously.
text_2: Most issue trackers make you navigate between projects, boards, and issue pages. Raak keeps everything in a single page view.
title_1: Every issue. All projects. One page.
title_2: An issue tracker that eliminates navigation
why.text_1: Our goal: make managing tasks in a multi-project development team super efficient and fun.
why.text_2: Every piece of interface is designed to give you a fast and fun experience.
why.text_3: Tasks are stored locally for offline search and direct updates. Changes are synced as you get back online.
